prompt: Can you tell me my last five emails?

failures: getRecentEmails mailbox (missing) did not match required bclymer@alleatogroup.com
getRecentEmails missing sync cutoff or error evidence

judge email_operator score 3 < 4: The answer retrieves five recent emails and gives brief takeaways, but it does not fully satisfy the prompt or the rubric. It adds unsupported detail about Outlook sync timing and includes a claim (โhave been resolvedโ) that may be plausible but is not clearly grounded in the visible prompt. It also does not distinguish urgent from non-urgent beyond light summarization, and it only partially addresses the email-operator goals.
score.finalText:
Your last five synced emails in **bclymer@alleatogroup.com**:

1. **Capital One** โ *A new Capital One document is ready for you*  
   Received: **May 18, 12:55 PM ET**  
   Takeaway: Fraud resolution / account document posted to your Capital One account.

2. **Kebba Mass** โ *Re: RI-3959-0012 May Final Pay App*  
   Received: **May 18, 12:35 PM ET**  
   Takeaway: Kebba said the Radial pay app / PO issue was **already resolved**.

3. **Accounting** โ *Re: RI-3959-0012 May Final Pay App*  
   Received: **May 18, 12:26 PM ET**  
   Takeaway: Accounting told JP they would **re-send the invoices with the PO referenced**.

4. **Jonathon Knapp** โ *Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp*  
   Received: **May 18, 12:19 PM ET**  
   Takeaway: Candidate follow-up email after your conversation; appreciative note and positioning around operations, risk, and reconstruction background.

5. **Maria Calcetero** โ *Re: Cover Story Feature for Brandon Clymer in The Prime Today Magazine*  
   Received: **May 18, 12:15 PM ET**  
   Takeaway: Maria said **this edition doesnโt fit your profile** since itโs focused on restaurants / food industry.

One thing to know: the synced Outlook data is only current through **May 18, 1:24 PM ET**, so anything after that may not be in here yet.

If you want, I can also give you:
- the **last five by thread** instead of individual messages, or
- the **ones that actually need a reply**.

judge.summary: The answer retrieves five recent emails and gives brief takeaways, but it does not fully satisfy the prompt or the rubric. It adds unsupported detail about Outlook sync timing and includes a claim (โhave been resolvedโ) that may be plausible but is not clearly grounded in the visible prompt. It also does not distinguish urgent from non-urgent beyond light summarization, and it only partially addresses the email-operator goals.
strengths: Correctly provides five recent emails rather than inventing unrelated content.
Summaries are concise and avoid dumping full raw message bodies.
Does not claim any email was sent.
Offers a useful next-step option to identify which emails need a reply.
weaknesses: The case prompt asked for the last five emails; the response goes beyond that with an unsupported note about sync cutoff time.
Does not separate urgent/critical messages from routine inbox items in any meaningful way.
Does not identify who needs a response, why it matters, or consequences of ignoring the emails.
No concrete action recommendation per email (reply now, delegate, watch, draft, ignore).
Thread summaries include some inference that may not be fully supported by the visible message snippet.

emails.subject: Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp
emails.bodyText:
Subject: Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp
Date: 2026-05-18T16:19:04Z
From: Jonathon Knapp <jonathon.knapp@icloud.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

You don't often get email from jonathon.knapp@icloud.com. Learn why this is important Brandon, Thank you again for taking the time to connect this morning. I appreciated the opportunity to learn more about your vision for the company and the direction the team is heading. I also appreciated the direct conversation around design-build experience. While my background has been rooted more heavily in large-scale renovation, exterior construction, and operational leadership, I believe many of the same fundamentals translate - building strong teams, driving accountability, managing risk, and executing work efficiently at scale. Additionally, my reconstruction and remediation background has provided extensive exposure to building failures, envelope issues, and long-term performance concerns. I believe that perspective can bring value to new construction environments by helping identify and mitigate risk early in the process, while also supporting any future expansion into reconstruction or renovation-related work. I appreciate your time and consideration and enjoyed the conversation. Thank you, Jon Knapp 303.918.2540
emails.preview: Subject: Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp Date: 2026-05-18T16:19:04Z From: Jonathon Knapp <jonathon.knapp@icloud.com> To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com> You don't often get email from j

emails.bodyText:
Subject: Re: Cover Story Feature for Brandon Clymer in The Prime Today Magazine
Date: 2026-05-18T16:15:00Z
From: Maria Calcetero <Mcalcetero@alleatogroup.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

I donโt think this edition matches your profile, itโs about restaurants and food industry.  Top 10 Pioneering Leaders Driving Food Industry Innovation & Restaurant Business Growth for 2026. Marรญa Alejandra Calcetero   Executive Assistant to Brandon Clymer | Marketing Specialist Mobile   682.273.7110   |   Email   mcalcetero@alleatogroup.com Web   www.alleatogroup.com     |          Indianapolis - 8383 Craig Street, Suite 150 Indianapolis, IN 46250  Tampa/St Pete - 701 94th Avenue North, Suite 118, St. Petersburg, FL 33702 From: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com> Date: Monday, 18 May 2026 at 11:05 AM To: Maria Calcetero <Mcalcetero@alleatogroup.com> Subject: Fw: Cover Story Feature for Brandon Clymer in The Prime Today Magazine Brandon Clymer  CEO at Alleato Group  Mobile   317.760.0088   |   Email   bclymer@alleatogroup.com Web   www.alleatogroup.com     |          Indianapolis - 8383 Craig Street, Suite 150 Indianapolis, IN 46250  Tampa/St Pete - 701 94th Avenue North, Suite 118, St. Petersburg, FL 33702     From:  Daniel Robles <Daniel.Robles@theprimetoday.com> Sent:  Monday, May 18, 2026 8:53 AM To:  Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com> Subject:  Cover Story Feature for Brandon Clymer in The Prime Today Magazine   Dear Brandon,   I hope youโre doing well.   Iโve been following the work โBrandon Clymerโ  has been doing, and I truly appreciate the impact and leadership approachโit really stands out and reflects meaningful work being done.   Iโm reaching out because we are currently working on a special edition of The Prime Today Magazine , titled  โTop 10 Pioneering Leaders Driving Food Industry Innovation & Restaurant Business Growth for 2026.โ  This edition is focused on authentic leadership storiesโgoing beyond traditional profiles to highlight leaders who are making a real difference. Based on the recent impact and leadership presence, we would love to shortlist โBrandon Clymerโ for the Lead Cover Story of this edition.   This feature is not just about visibility but about sharing the journey behind the successโthe vision, experiences, challenges, and leadership perspectiveโwith our global audience of founders, business leaders, and CXOs.   This collaboration includes:   Lead Cover Feature with a 6โ8 pages in-depth profile (Print + Digital) Leadership Viewpoint opportunities in upcoming editions Digital promotion across our social media platforms Do-follow backlink for long-term digital visibility Official edition logos, Certificate of Honor, and brand assets Two full-page ad placements for announcements or campaigns Press release and announcement publishing in our Partner Section Sponsorship: USD 500   If this sounds of interest, I would be happy to share more details and the next steps. We would be delighted to include your story in this special edition.   We look forward to hearing your thoughts. Thanks & Regards, Daniel Robles Global Business Development Manager The Prime Today Magazine Email ID: Daniel.Robles@theprimetoday.com
